title: Dual-Agent Stability: Hermes Supervisor + OpenClaw Executor
description: Cross-cutting pattern for maximum stability using Hermes as a supervisor/self-healing layer over OpenClaw. Eliminates single point of failure, adds learning loop for improving recovery time, and reduces costs through model tiering.
core_principle: OpenClaw executes. Hermes watches, reviews, learns, and heals. Mutual health monitoring = zero single point of failure.
key_rules: Health check interval: 30 seconds minimum (cheaper models, minimal token cost)
Failure threshold: 3 consecutive failures before escalation (avoid false alarms)
[ACK] is terminal — never reply to it
Max 3 message turns per exchange between agents
Cost tiering: Hermes on Haiku/cheap model ($0.25-$1/day), OpenClaw stays on Opus
Hermes learning loop improves recovery time over repeated incidents (40s → 11s documented)
Run Hermes as system service (systemd/launchd) for crash recovery of Hermes itself
Keep OpenClaw as primary for multi-agent orchestration (Auditor, RCA, etc.)
